example_profile_summary: 35, retirement in ~30 years, 10k lump sum + 500/mo, risk 7/10

recommendation.explanation: To help you reach your retirement goal over the next 30 years, I have designed a portfolio focused on long-term growth. This portfolio is composed entirely of stock ETFs, which allow you to participate in company profits and historically offer the highest long-term returns, though their value can fluctuate in the short term [1, 2, 3]. Specifically, we utilize the iShares Core S&P 500 ETF and the Vanguard FTSE Developed Markets Index Fund to provide broad global exposure [1, 2]. We also include the Vanguard ESG U.S. Stock ETF to prioritize companies with strong environmental, social, and governance practices [3]. These funds were selected for their cost-efficient structure, with expense ratios as low as 0.03% [1, 2, 3]. As a helpful starting point, we suggest an initial investment of 9,000 EUR supplemented by a 500 EUR monthly savings plan. If your comfort with market fluctuations changes, please let me know, and we can adjust the risk level of your portfolio.

sources.text:
# iShares Core S&P 500 ETF (IVV)

Data as of 2026-07-08. Sources: fund metadata from the project universe, live fundamentals and 5-year price history via Yahoo Finance.

## What this fund is

This is an equity (stock) ETF. Equity funds participate in company profits and historically offer the highest long-term returns, but their value can fluctuate strongly in the short term.

iShares Core S&P 500 ETF trades under the ticker IVV in the category 'Large Blend'.
Fund size: 888.1 billion USD.

## Costs

The total expense ratio (TER) is 0.03% per year. Costs are deducted from the fund's assets automatically; lower cost means more of the market return stays with the investor.

## Risk profile (computed from 5-year price history)

- Annualized volatility: 17.0%
- Annualized return: 13.3%
- Maximum drawdown: -24.5%
- Sharpe ratio: 0.82
